sketch0.title: Introduction to Text Concatenation in SQL

intro-concat.title: Introduction to Text Concatenation in SQL
intro-concat.bodyMarkdown:
Concatenation means joining two or more text values together to form a single string. In SQL, you can concatenate columns or string literals using the `||` operator (in SQLite) or the `CONCAT()` function (in some other databases). For example, if you want to combine a customer's `full_name` and `city` into one string, you can write:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ', ' || city AS name_and_city
FROM customers;
```
This will produce results like `Mia Lopez, Chicago`.

concat-null-behavior.title: Handling NULLs in Concatenation
concat-null-behavior.bodyMarkdown:
When concatenating columns, if any value is `NULL`, the result will also be `NULL`. For example, if a customer does not have an email, concatenating their name and email will result in `NULL`. To avoid this, you can use the `COALESCE()` function to provide a default value:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' <' || COALESCE(email, 'no-email') || '>' AS contact_info
FROM customers;
```
This ensures that even if `email` is `NULL`, the string will still be complete.

concat-use-cases.title: Common Use Cases for Concatenation
concat-use-cases.bodyMarkdown: Concatenation is useful for formatting output, creating display names, or generating custom labels. For example, you might want to create a mailing label by combining the customer's name and city, or format an email display as `Name <email>`.

single-choice-concat-operator.title: Which operator is used for concatenating text in SQLite?
single-choice-concat-operator.prompt: Suppose you want to combine the `full_name` and `city` columns in the `customers` table. Which operator should you use in SQLite?

help.concept: In SQLite, the double pipe `||` is the standard operator for joining text values together.
help.hint_1: Look for the operator that is specifically for string concatenation, not arithmetic.
help.hint_2: The double vertical bar is used for this purpose in SQLite.

multi-choice-concat-use-cases.title: When is text concatenation useful?
multi-choice-concat-use-cases.prompt: Select all situations where concatenating text fields in SQL is helpful.
multi-choice-concat-use-cases.hint: Think about formatting and combining text, not calculations or filtering.

help.concept: Concatenation is for joining text fields, often for display or formatting purposes.
help.hint_1: It's not used for mathematical operations or filtering.
help.hint_2: Look for options that involve combining or formatting text.

options.a: Creating a display name from first and last name
options.b: Calculating the sum of two numeric columns
options.d: Filtering rows based on a condition

drag-reorder-concat-syntax.title: Arrange the SQL for concatenating name and city
drag-reorder-concat-syntax.prompt: Put the following SQL tokens in order to select the customer's full name and city, separated by a comma and space.
drag-reorder-concat-syntax.hint: Start with the correct answer, then concatenate the columns with the operator.

help.concept: The concatenation operator joins text columns and literals in the the correct answer clause.
help.hint_1: Remember to use the operator between each part you want to join.
help.hint_2: The alias comes after the concatenated expression, before the correct answer.

fill-blank-choice-concat-null.title: Fill in the blank: Handling NULLs in concatenation
fill-blank-choice-concat-null.prompt: Which SQL function can you use to replace NULL values when concatenating text fields?
fill-blank-choice-concat-null.hint: It's a function that returns the first non-NULL value.

help.concept: the missing term returns the first non-NULL argument, making it useful for replacing NULLs in concatenation.
help.hint_2: This function is commonly used to provide fallback values for NULLs.

fill-blank-choice-concat-null.template: SELECT full_name || ' <' || ______(email, 'no-email') || '>' AS contact_info FROM customers;

code-input-concat-email-label.title: Write a query to format customer email labels
code-input-concat-email-label.prompt: Write a SQL query that selects `full_name` and `email` from the `customers` table, and creates a new column called `label` that combines them in the format: `Full Name <email>`. If `email` is NULL, use `no-email` instead.
code-input-concat-email-label.hint: Use the concatenation operator and COALESCE for the email.

help.concept: Combine columns and string literals using the concatenation operator, and use COALESCE to handle NULL values.
help.hint_1: Concatenate full_name, a space and angle bracket, and the email (with COALESCE).
help.hint_2: Remember to alias the new column as 'label' and use COALESCE(email, 'no-email').
